obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Page background, card surface, filled button fill — the flat black ground that all white typography and outlines sit on

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text, pill button borders, link text, section dividers, card hover overlays — every outlined and typographic element

ash.$description: Ash — Muted borders and secondary structural strokes where pure white would compete with primary controls

lausanne.$description: The single voice — used for display headings (72px/1.00), section labels, body text, pill labels, and navigation. Weight 400 at 72px is a deliberate anti-convention: headlines whisper into the dark rather than shout, gaining authority through geometric restraint instead of boldness. The tight 1.0 line-height at display size creates blocky architectural headlines.

victor-serif.$description: Rare editorial accent — appears as italic or contrast voice at body size. A serif breaking the sans-only system signals 'this text is different' (timestamps, quotes, personal notes). Used sparingly enough that each appearance is noticed.

black-tie.$description: Decorative display face for one-off moments — a monospaced or display serif/script that breaks rhythm. The system is nearly all Lausanne, so a single Black Tie appearance acts as typographic spice.

obsidian-field.$description: Surface level 0: Full-bleed page background — the single flat stage

bone-plate.$description: Surface level 1: Inverse surface for image fills and right-column content; also the text/border color on the dark side
